behaviors.description: Renamed files appear in their own category and are not also counted as a deletion plus an addition.
behaviors.justification: Rationale A (failure boundary): rename classification depends on real Git rename detection and CLI/repo state, not just one local function. Rationale B (infra): this is exercised through real repo + git command behavior (often subprocess), and mocks can hide rename-threshold/format differences. Rejected lower level: Unit is disqualified because behavior text requires category correctness from repository analysis; Integration is disqualified because real git/subprocess semantics are the risk.
behaviors.test_description: Create a real temp git repo, commit a file, rename it, run the CLI, and assert report shows exactly one rename and no paired add/delete for that path.

behaviors.description: Diffs that include binary files do not cause the tool to crash; binary files are counted in the output.
behaviors.justification: Rationale A (failure boundary): binary diff handling failures usually appear when parsing real git diff output/bytes and process streams. Rationale B (mock-hide): stubbed text diffs can pass while real binary markers/encoding and subprocess output trigger crashes. Rejected lower level: Integration is disqualified because real subprocess/filesystem binary behavior is central to the failure mode.
behaviors.test_description: In a real temp repo, commit then modify/add a binary file, run the CLI, assert exit success and binary change included in counts.

behaviors.description: Running the tool on a repository that has no commits yet produces an empty report rather than an error.
behaviors.justification: Rationale A (failure boundary): unborn-HEAD behavior is a real git repository state edge case. Rationale B (infra): tools commonly receive specific git exit codes/messages here; in-process fakes often miss this. Rejected lower level: Integration is disqualified because the key risk is real git/repo/subprocess handling of no-commit state.
behaviors.test_description: Initialize a real git repo with no commits, run the CLI, assert successful execution and empty structured report.

behaviors.description: The counts in the report match the actual change history of the repository being analyzed.
behaviors.justification: Rationale A (failure boundary): correctness is end-to-end against actual git history semantics across adds/deletes/modifies/renames. Rationale B (mock-hide): fixtures/mocks can mirror implementation assumptions and miss drift from real git output/history traversal. Rejected lower level: Integration is disqualified because validating against real repository history is the behavior itself.
behaviors.test_description: Build a real repo history with known numbers of added/deleted/renamed/modified files across commits, run CLI, and assert report counts equal ground-truth from repository state.

behaviors.description: The output is identical whether the changes being analyzed are staged or unstaged.
behaviors.justification: Rationale A (failure boundary): staged vs unstaged depends on real index/worktree behavior and git query mode selection. Rationale B (infra): only real repo/index interactions reliably expose mismatches in command flags and parsing. Rejected lower level: Integration is disqualified because the behavior explicitly compares real git states that are infrastructure-backed.
behaviors.test_description: In a real temp repo, create equivalent staged-only and unstaged-only change scenarios, run CLI for each, and assert identical structured output.
